candidates.stmt:
I'm Mark and I'm proud to be a resident of the Borough since the age of two weeks. I went to St Paul's, Haslewood (until they merged into st caths) and John Warner. Learnt to swim at the old lido. Had my first paying job here at Pindar road and despite the millennial "difficulty mode" at life I'm also a proud homeowner in hoddesdon.

I've been involved with the community as a teenager since my time with East Herts Scout with events such as the deconsfield fate, and in recent years the local Conservative party which I do believe wholeheartedly has been fantastic for the Borough despite the parliamentary picture in the last few years. As someone who has benefited from the local services (especially the local SEND provision) my main mission is to give back to the community.

There's the "why", now the "How"! The mission is simple, low taxes and good services. The council has maintained one of the lowest borough council tax in the country (only a extra 10p a week for Band D properties this year) whilst keeping to the biweekly bin collection routine, regular gardening of public spaces (there has been comments on this in my canvassing sessions, has not been forgotten!), local council owned leisure faculties such as John Warner and Laura Trott sports centres and events like rock around the clock in hoddesdon. We want to continue on this route and where there's issues, your councillors will always hear you and have your back.

If you have any concerns please get in touch, as campaigning season is getting into full swing I'm out most afternoon delivering materials in the ward so if you see me feel welcome to say hi and have a chat!

Sally Kemp has lived in Broxbourne Borough all her life, attending school in Cheshunt before studying economics at Newcastle University. Her main career interest has been primary teaching, as a special needs coordinator and organiser of environmental studies. She has also served as a school governor. Sally's particular local concern is preserving open spaces in the borough both for local people to enjoy and to enhance biodiversity.
I live in the Borough of Broxbourne and feel it is important for everyone to be able to vote for a representative of the party they want to.So I am happy to stand as a Lib Dem and offer that option.

Kostas is of Greek and Ukranian heritage and, over 50 years ago at the age of seven came to live with his father in North London. By the age of 22 he had started his own business in ladies fashion, was married with four children, and had bought his own house in Cheshunt. Two years ago he retired and celebrated with his children and grandchildren in that same house. Every one of his children and grandchildren has attended the same two schools - Brooklands and Turnford.

I have lived in Cheshunt for nearly a decade and have grown deeply attached to the community and its people. My professional background is in education, as I’m a former primary school teacher and I now work for a local charity dedicated to supporting vulnerable residents. In addition to my work, I volunteer with Cheshunt Library, where I help run the Home Library Service for residents in both Cheshunt and Waltham Cross. I also play an active role in the Summer Reading Challenge, encouraging young people to develop a lifelong love of books and learning.

My involvement with Broxbourne Labour began out of a deep frustration with years of Conservative complacency and neglect. I’ve seen first-hand the damage caused by a steady decline in public services, the ongoing cost of living crisis, and the growing sense that ordinary people are being left behind. I joined Labour because I believe in building a fairer, more compassionate society, one that listens to its residents and invests in their futures. I want to be part of a movement that delivers real change for our community and restores a sense of hope and progress.

Florine has lived in Broxbourne for a decade. She is passionate about making Cheshunt a safe and clean place for good health and quality of life. As a mum of young children who also has ageing parents, she knows that the cost of living and access to good local services matter.

I'm 25, always actively thinking about the future, of my own and younger generations who will face climate and social struggles. I believe this starts with building intentional community. I'm a freelance theatre producer, artist whose always lived in Cheshunt that is and wants others brave enough to care.

My name is Shenis Hassan, and I'm a local Goffs Oak resident seeking to become one of your local councillors this year.

I have always cared deeply about where I live, which has led to being involved in local volunteering and the campaign to stop the development on our green space, working closely with Broxbourne Council, Local Council Leader, Cllr Corina Gander and our MP, Lewis Cocking. After a fiercely contested battle we managed to stop the developer from building over some of our critical green spaces, which are loved by so many.

I love being part of the community and meeting my neighbours and I am an active member of the Goff’s Oak Community Association. We all accept that new housing is needed but in the right place locally and respecting the wider community’s needs.

I intend to play a key role in ensuring development is appropriate and that developers work with closely with us. I recently presented at the planning and regulatory committee to make sure our community is clearly heard as they consider the Laurel Park development.

To continue the community work further and to save our green space, I will be standing in the local elections, and have enjoyed meeting with local people on the doorstep in recent weeks and months.

I encourage you to reach out and speak to me, as I am dedicated to meeting and speaking with every resident across the Goffs Oak ward.

I have lived in Goffs Oak for ten years and work locally in our family business. I am really passionate about supporting small businesses which are huge contributors to the local economy and community. I also believe local politics should focus on listening to residents and protecting what makes our community special.

I am standing because I want to see our Green Belt and village character genuinely defended, fly-tipping tackled properly rather than just cleared up, and a neighbourhood where every resident feels welcome and respected. Goffs Oak is a community for everyone who calls it home. Vote for hope on May 7th!

I have lived in Broxbourne for over 40 years and I am shocked by the way our local environment has been allowed to deteriorate. Local services  (Schools, Doctors Surgeries, Recreation) are under pressure whilst remaining Green Belt is being concreted over by poorly planned developments. The local elections in May is an opportunity to shake up the local Council and to send a message that enough is enough. Even if you do not usually vote Lib Dem, please consider lending us your vote to send a message that the voters of Goffs Oak will not be taken for granted.

Although James grew up in Shepherd’s Bush, he has proudly called Broxbourne home since 2010. He has built his career in senior partnership and strategy roles across the telecoms and retail sectors, bringing a practical, results-driven approach to everything he does.

While studying Environmental Economics as part of his Master’s degree, James developed a strong belief that a thriving economy and a greener future go hand in hand. He understands that sustainable growth isn’t a trade-off — it’s an opportunity.

Locally, James is passionate about preserving Broxbourne’s open spaces and improving public transport, making the area an even better place to live, work, and raise a family.

There’s a saying: “You are a product of your environment.” James believes this applies at every level. By improving the environment in Broxbourne and beyond, we can create stronger communities and a better quality of life for everyone.

Hoddesdon represents the best of our British values.

But we must ask ourselves, is the council doing enough?

Broxbourne has seen much change over the 52 years of Conservative leadership. We now approach a point in time where it is more important than ever to protect the place that we hold deep in our hearts.

As the Conservatives stray from conservatism, we must do all that we can to protect our beloved green belt, our public spaces & our local businesses.

The council protects its tax purse, asking for more annually whilst delivering passable services. It's stale and it's inefficient.

Let's make a change. As a dedicated and ambitious individual, I'll strive to allocate funding to the right areas of Hoddesdon Town, to put an end to council tax waste and commit to transparency.

All my very best,
Giorgio Daniel

I am proud to stand as your Green Party candidate for Hoddesdon Town and Rye Park. Originally from Barnsley, I moved to the Broxbourne area in 2022 after graduating from the University of Nottingham with a degree in Chemistry. For over three years, I have lived and worked locally as a research chemist. I have a professional scientific background and want to bring these skills to Broxbourne Borough Council. I joined the Green Party because I believe it is the only party with the vision to improve people’s lives sustainably. I am committed to prioritising long-term social good over short-term gain.

Key Issues:
Affordable Housing: With the new Local Plan review beginning this year, I will push for developments that prioritize genuinely affordable homes for local people, ensuring they meet high environmental standards to keep energy bills low.

Road Safety: As someone who walks to work locally, I see the danger posed by speeding firsthand. I will advocate for more pedestrian crossings and speed cameras in high-risk areas to ensure our streets are safe for everyone.

I joined the Labour Party to make a real difference. Growing up, I was a young carer until the age of 15, when I tragically lost my mum to a failing NHS and a broken social care system. I saw first-hand how chronic underfunding devastated SEND support in our schools. Those experience shaped my life, and I swore I’d fight tooth and nail to make sure no child or family has to go through what mine did.

That’s why I’m proud to be standing as a candidate in the very community where I grew up, where I went to school, and where I witnessed the strength, resilience, and hard work that built Hoddesdon. If elected as your Labour councillor, I will work to restore and empower our community through a new era of co-operativism—putting people before profit, and rebuilding from the ground.

I live in the Hoddesdon Town and Rye Park ward and am very passionate about our town and surroundings. I volunteered for the Neighbourhood Watch as the ward coordinator for 15 years, I am also a member of 'The Friends of Lowewood' and 'The Hoddesdon Society'. I am a third generation Hoddesdonian and love living in our town. I believe that our town and our borough deserve a stronger voice for our residents and that resources could be better utilised to meet our collective needs, both across our ward and our constituency.

I believe that our council is not doing enough with regards to:

* Protecting our green spaces and ensuring local infrastructure is fit for purpose

* Scrutinising council spending and having more robust financial projections

* Working to tackle anti-social behaviour and supporting vulnerable youngsters to find positive influences and constructive activities

I want to listen to my fellow Hoddesdonians, so that I can work on what is important for all of us.

I have lived in Cheshunt all my life and care deeply about the future of our community. I am standing as a Green Party candidate because I believe local politics should focus on supporting residents, bringing people together, and protecting the environment we all rely on.

I work in IT in the public transport sector, supporting essential public services, and understand the importance of practical, reliable systems that work for everyday people. Outside of work, I enjoy walking and bird watching and regularly visit local nature reserves, which has strengthened my commitment to protecting green spaces.

I am standing to offer residents a positive and compassionate alternative in local politics, with a focus on protecting green spaces, tackling fly tipping, improving local services, and supporting safer neighbourhoods in Rosedale and Bury Green.

I believe local representatives should be approachable, listen to residents, and work constructively to improve the community.

I have lived in Cheshunt for nearly 11 years and I now work in corporate affairs for a national trade association representing the British businesses underpinned by heritage crafts, a vital sector that supports over half a million skilled workers and contributes £81 billion to our economy. These businesses, rooted in generations of tradition, not only preserve our cultural identity but also serve as a powerful tool of British soft power on the world stage. I was born in Romania, but raised with traditional British values of hard work, fairness, and community. values that are deeply reflected in the craft sector. It’s a privilege to advocate for industries that keep these values alive and pass them on through skilled, meaningful employment across the country.

I joined the Labour Party in 2017, driven by a growing frustration with the deepening inequalities in society and the continued erosion of our public services under Conservative leadership. I believe passionately that government should invest in the things that make a real, positive difference in people’s lives: from strong local services to affordable housing, accessible healthcare, and secure, meaningful jobs. Everyone deserves the opportunity to thrive, not just the wealthy few. For me, Labour is the party best placed to rebuild a fairer, more inclusive Britain, one where prosperity is shared and no one is left behind.

Having grown up in the community, Owen understands the issues local residents face and is committed to being a strong local voice.
He is standing as a Green Party candidate because he believes local politics should focus on supporting residents and protecting the places that matter most to the community.

Owen is standing to offer residents a positive alternative in local politics, with a focus on protecting green spaces, tackling fly tipping, improving local services and supporting safer neighbourhoods in Waltham Cross. A key priority for Owen is road safety. As a supporter of the 20’s Plenty campaign, he wants to reduce the danger caused by speeding on residential streets and would work as a councillor to promote safer speed limits and practical measures that protect residents, especially children and older people.

He believes local representatives should listen to residents and work constructively to improve the community. Owen’s campaign reflects the Green Party’s commitment to putting people first.

My name is Fabio Bonfante and I have lived in Waltham Cross for almost all of my life.

Whilst I count myself lucky to live here, I am becoming increasingly concerned with many issues facing my town and my borough. Sadly, with the area being a ‘safe zone’ for the Conservatives, as long as I have lived here, Waltham Cross in particular is paid very little attention by any level of local government.

Congestion on Broxbourne’s roads is becoming unmanageable, particularly in Waltham Cross and Cheshunt. It is increasingly difficult to do basic things such as get to the shops or drop children off to school. Sometimes a ten minute journey across the borough can take an hour! We have seen a building boom over the last few years, with the Conservative council approving more and more new large scale developments, but without accompanying improvement to our our roads and transport systems. Our bus network is patchy and infrequent, leaving some of our most vulnerable people unable to move about. 

Our rail links into London are vital for our local economy and commuters in Waltham Cross deserve more frequent and better quality services in to and out of London, I want to see our roads and public transport properly planned for, looked after and funded, not neglected. 

Whilst it is important that we build affordable and decent homes for our residents, development has to be sustainable and put the needs of locals first, this is something I want to fight hard for. I recently spoke with some of the elderly in Broxbourne, who told me that they remember a time when the Conservatives had an opposition in the council; I and my fellow Broxbourne Liberal Democrats want to be that opposition and an effective voice to oppose Conservative plans when they don’t represent the interests of our residents.

I want to contribute to our area, rather than just complain, which is why I am standing for election as your next borough Councillor in May.

I want to get the very best for our ar

Helen (Ellie) Austen has lived and worked in Broxbourne for more than 20 years.  She is an active volunteer with two community garden schemes in the Broxbourne area.  As a teacher she is concerned with greening the curriculum.  She is also working to improve access to an appropriate school place for all members of our local population.

I’ve been proud to call Cheshunt home for over nine years. In that time, I’ve witnessed how much our community has changed, sometimes for the better, but all too often, vital services have been stretched thin while local voices have gone unheard. I’ve always had a deep interest in politics, but it was the last general election that inspired me to take a more active role in local political life. I believe that change begins at the community level, and that we each have a responsibility to speak up and get involved in shaping a better future for everyone.

One of my core priorities is working with local organisations to end violence against women and girls. Broxbourne must be a place where women feel safe, supported, and heard. That means creating a dedicated safe haven for domestic abuse victims, strengthening the laws that protect them, and properly funding the support services they rely on. But just as crucially, we must invest in education — in our schools, homes, and wider community — to break the cycle of abuse before it begins. Every woman deserves safety, justice, and respect. By coming together as a community, we can build a Broxbourne where those values are more than just words — they’re a lived reality.
